ground_truth.title: Bryant Park Yoga
ground_truth.main_content:
Everyone's favorite yoga program returns for summer 2025! From May 28 through September 17, join us for classes Tuesday mornings on the Upper Terrace, and Wednesday evenings on the lawn. Bryant Park Yoga presented by Halara is free to the public. Bring your mat and water and enjoy one hour of yoga with New York City's top instructors. Please register for each class at the links below!

2025 Instructors - Tuesdays, 10am: Jun 3 - Dre Drummond, Jun 10 - Kanan Kapila (cancelled), Jun 17 - Ingrid A. Marcroft, Jun 24 - Kristen Kemp, Jul 1 - Andrew Pyo, Jul 8 - Loryn Riggiola, Jul 15 - Nico Sarani, Jul 22 - Phyllicia Bonanno, Jul 29 - Kosha Rakholia, Aug 5 - Ruah Bhay, Aug 12 - Mona Anand, Aug 19 - Amanda Gloria Valdes, Aug 26 - Kate Posch, Sep 2 - Kim Bauman, Sep 9 - Yuval Samburski, Sep 16 - Zack Thompson.

Wednesdays, 6pm: May 28 - Nico Sarani (cancelled), Jun 4 - Bee Creel, Jun 11 - Angelica Wilson, Jun 18 - Pilin Anice, Jun 25 - Bethany Lyons, Jul 2 - Sarah Platt-Finger, Jul 9 - Anna Greenberg, Jul 16 - No class, Jul 23 - Kirra Michel, Jul 30 - Lulu Soni, Aug 6 - Colleen Saidman Yee + Rodney Yee, Aug 13 - Neeti Narula, Aug 20 - Kristin McGee, Aug 27 - Jo Murdock, Sep 3* - Mariana Fernández, Sep 10 - Erica Chen, Sep 17 - Kim Copeland. *Class taught in Spanish and English.

Registration is required. Register for Wednesday classes or Tuesday classes online or in person. Pre-registration is strongly encouraged. Those who pre-register opt in to receive updates on weather-related cancellations and other notifications. By pre-registering, you complete the waiver in advance and will receive weather-related updates. All Bryant Park Yoga classes are free and open to the public! All levels are welcome, thanks to the skill of our experienced teachers.

Onsite check-in: Please present your QR code from your registration confirmation email to the Bryant Park check-in staff. Check-in locations can be found at all four corners of the lawn. Each check-in location is marked with pink barricades or a pink tent. We recommend you arrive at least 15 minutes early so you have time to check-in and roll out your mat before instruction begins.

What to bring: Your own yoga mat (the park does not provide mats), water to keep hydrated during class, and a towel if needed.

Livestream: We are thrilled to continue offering livestream sessions Wednesday evenings at 6pm on Instagram @bryantparknyc. Follow along live or return later to view past classes!

Weather: Classes are cancelled only when it is deemed unsafe or uncomfortable for participants. Classes may also be moved to a new location or cancelled if holding class could harm the lawn. Follow @bryantparknyc for day-of updates. Pre-registered participants will also receive weather-related updates via email.
